Rapuntium maculatum, also known as the Stinking Harebell, is a flowering plant in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae). The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Rapuntium maculatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Rapuntium maculatum include teas and infusions, which are often used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
